prompt: The notation ⊨ φ (with no premises on the left) means:
options: φ can be derived using the proof rules
φ is true in every model (it is valid)
hint: ⊨ is the SEMANTIC turnstile; ⊢ is the SYNTACTIC (provability) one.
explanation: ⊨ φ is the semantic turnstile: φ is true under every interpretation/model — i.e. φ is valid. Contrast ⊢ φ (the syntactic turnstile), which means φ is provable from the axioms and rules. Soundness and completeness are precisely the claims that these two coincide.
